_meta.description: Jewish calendar, festivals, and sacred times as recorded in Scripture
_meta.note: Dates given in Hebrew calendar months

months.notes: Seventh month; first month of civil year
Twelfth month; Adar II added in leap years

festivals.name: Passover (Pesach)
festivals.purpose: Commemorates the exodus from Egypt; the passing over of Israelite homes
observances: Slaughter of Passover lamb at twilight
Lamb roasted and eaten with unleavened bread and bitter herbs
No bone of the lamb to be broken

festivals.name: Unleavened Bread (Chag HaMatzot)
festivals.purpose: Commemorates the hasty departure from Egypt; no time for bread to rise
observances: Eat unleavened bread for seven days
Sacred assemblies on first and seventh days
No regular work on first and seventh days

festivals.name: Firstfruits (Bikkurim)
festivals.date: Nisan 16 (day after Sabbath during Unleavened Bread)
festivals.purpose: Presentation of the first sheaf of barley harvest to the LORD
observances: Wave a sheaf of firstfruits before the LORD
Burnt offering of a male lamb
Grain offering with oil and drink offering
No new grain eaten until offering made

festivals.name: Weeks/Pentecost (Shavuot)
festivals.purpose: Celebration of wheat harvest; later associated with giving of the Law at Sinai
observances: Wave offering of two loaves of leavened bread
Leave gleanings for poor and foreigner

festivals.name: Trumpets (Yom Teruah)
festivals.purpose: Day of trumpet blasts; beginning of civil new year (later Rosh Hashanah)

festivals.name: Day of Atonement (Yom Kippur)
festivals.purpose: Annual atonement for sins of priests and people; cleansing of the sanctuary
observances: High priest enters Most Holy Place with blood
Two goats: one sacrificed, one (scapegoat) sent into wilderness

festivals.name: Tabernacles/Booths (Sukkot)
festivals.purpose: Commemorates dwelling in booths during wilderness wanderings; harvest thanksgiving
observances: Dwell in booths (sukkot) for seven days
Take branches of palm, willow, and leafy trees
Sacred assembly on first day; no regular work

festivals.name: Sabbath (Shabbat)
festivals.purpose: Day of rest; commemorates God's rest after creation and deliverance from Egypt
observances: Sign of the covenant between God and Israel

festivals.name: New Moon (Rosh Chodesh)
festivals.purpose: Marking the beginning of each month

festivals.name: Sabbath Year (Shemitah)
festivals.purpose: Rest for the land; release of debts
observances: What grows naturally is for all to eat

festivals.name: Year of Jubilee (Yovel)
festivals.purpose: Liberty proclaimed; land returns to original owners; slaves freed
observances: Trumpet blast on Day of Atonement
Return of property to original families

post_exilic_festivals.name: Purim
post_exilic_festivals.purpose: Commemorates deliverance of Jews in Persia through Esther and Mordecai
observances: Reading of the book of Esther
Giving gifts to one another and to the poor

post_exilic_festivals.name: Hanukkah (Dedication)
post_exilic_festivals.purpose: Commemorates rededication of the temple after Maccabean victory (167 BC)
observances: Lighting of lamps/candles for eight days
post_exilic_festivals.origin: Instituted during Second Temple period (not in Torah)
post_exilic_festivals.note: Referenced as 'Feast of Dedication' in the NT; detailed in 1-2 Maccabees (apocrypha)
